channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Rosa Trustee Application Pack: How to Apply for Trustee Roles at the UK Fund for Women and Girls (2026)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Are you passionate about women’s rights, equality and social justice in the UK? Do you want to influence strategic decisions, governance, and funding that support women-led organisations across the country? Rosa UK Fund for Women and Girls is recruiting new Trustees – and this is your definitive guide to applying! Why Become a Trustee at […]

The post Rosa Trustee Application Pack: How to Apply for Trustee Roles at the UK Fund for Women and Girls (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about women’s rights, equality and social justice in the UK? Do you want to influence strategic decisions, governance, and funding that support women-led organisations across the country? Rosa UK Fund for Women and Girls is recruiting new Trustees – and this is your definitive guide to applying!

Why Become a Trustee at Rosa?

Becoming a Trustee at Rosa puts you at the heart of an organisation dedicated to building a thriving, resilient women and girls sector – especially organisations that are often underfunded and overlooked.

As a Trustee you will:

  • Help set strategic direction and purpose for the charity.

  • Ensure legal, financial and governance compliance.

  • Support senior staff and connect Rosa to broader networks.

  • Champion causes that improve women’s lives across the UK.

This isn’t just governance – it’s impactful leadership in the women’s sector.

Who Can Apply – Qualifications & Eligibility

While Trustees are volunteers, Rosa is looking for candidates who:

  • Share a commitment to women and girls’ equality.

  • Can bring skills in grant-making, fundraising, communications, financial governance or strategy.

  • May have private sector experience or governance knowledge.

  • Can contribute to an inclusive and diverse board – especially people from underrepresented backgrounds.

No previous Trustee experience is required – Rosa welcomes new leaders who are committed and strategic.

Trustee Responsibilities – What You’ll Do

Trustees help ensure Rosa is well-run and impactful. Typical responsibilities include:

1. Strategic & Governance Leadership

  • Provide oversight of organisational strategy and performance.

  • Ensure compliance with charity law and regulatory standards.

  • Review and refine policies that protect beneficiaries and staff.

2. Operational Support

  • Participate in quarterly board meetings and sub-committee work.

  • Act as ambassadors for Rosa in your professional and community networks.

  • Offer guidance on fundraising, communications, financial oversight or programme evaluation.

3. Governance Culture

  • Uphold values of integrity, independence and accountability.

  • Support a transparent and inclusive board culture.

Benefits of Serving as a Rosa Trustee

Being a Trustee is voluntary and unpaid, but comes with rich non-financial benefits:

1. Professional Growth

  • Deep governance and leadership experience

  • Exposure to high-level strategy and nonprofit funding dynamics

2. Network Expansion

  • Connections in the women’s sector, philanthropy, public policy and civil society

  • Opportunity to work with senior leaders and influencers

3. Personal Impact

  • Contribution to systemic change for women and girls across the UK

  • Meaningful role in shaping funding, policy and access to services

How to Apply – Step-by-Step

1.Download & Review the Trustee Info Pack

Visit Rosa’s website trustee page for a full application pack, role description and governance documents:
Click here

2. Prepare Your Application

Applications typically require:

  • A CV or résumé highlighting relevant experience

  • A cover letter explaining:

    • Why you want to be a Rosa Trustee

    • What skills you bring to the board

  • Answers to any specific questions in the application form

3. Submit Before Deadline

 Complete the online application form via Rosa’s website before 2nd April 2026.

Key Deadlines (2026 Trustee Recruitment Timeline)

The recruitment timetable from Rosa’s Trustee recruitment listing includes:

Milestone Date
Application Deadline 5pm, Thursday 2nd April 2026
First Interviews (Remote) 29th & 30th April 2026
Second Interviews (In-Person in London) 12th May 2026
Board Strategy Day / First Meeting 30th June 2026
All applicants must be UK-based.

Final Tips for a Winning Application

• Align your cover letter with Rosa’s mission and values.
• Highlight governance, leadership or sector experience.
• Be clear about your motivations for serving as Trustee.
• Reach out for an informal chat before applying to clarify expectations.

Joining the Board of Trustees at Rosa is a rare opportunity to lend your skills and voice to an organisation at the forefront of championing women’s rights and equality in the UK.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Rosa Trustee Application Pack: How to Apply for Trustee Roles at the UK Fund for Women and Girls (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nigeria Youth Futures Fund – Empowering Tomorrow’s Leaders With Grants, Training and Policy Impact
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Are you a young Nigerian change-maker, student, activist, or organization ready to transform your community and career? The Nigeria Youth Futures Fund is now open with life-changing opportunities designed to fund leadership, civic engagement, policy advocacy, and youth-led initiatives across Nigeria. What Is the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF)? The Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF) is […]

The post Nigeria Youth Futures Fund – Empowering Tomorrow’s Leaders With Grants, Training and Policy Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a young Nigerian change-maker, student, activist, or organization ready to transform your community and career? The Nigeria Youth Futures Fund is now open with life-changing opportunities designed to fund leadership, civic engagement, policy advocacy, and youth-led initiatives across Nigeria.

What Is the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF)?

The Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF) is a strategic initiative seeded by international partners like the MacArthur Foundation, Ford Foundation, and Luminate Group. Its mission is to empower, equip, and fund Nigerian youths and youth-led organizations to become effective leaders, advocates, and agents of sustainable social change within the country.

NYFF achieves this through grant funding, capacity-building, technical support, policy engagements, and a vibrant youth community ecosystem.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

NYFF offers opportunities for both individuals and organizations that meet the criteria below:

1. Youth Leadership Development Programme (YLDP)

To qualify for this flagship training and grant preparation programme:

  • Must be an undergraduate student at a Nigerian tertiary institution;

  • Must be between 18–35 years old;

  • Passionate about community development, advocacy, leadership, policy engagement;

  • Must have student ID and course form (for verification);

  • Must commit to the full duration of the programme.

2. NYFF Grants (Youth Leadership Development Fund)

For individuals or youth-led organizations seeking funding:

Individuals:

  • Nigerian citizens actively working on youth-focused projects;

  • Must show track record in leadership, activism, or community projects;

Organizations:

  • Must be registered with CAC or relevant state bodies;

  • Must be youth-led and working on sustainable solutions;

  • Minimum 60% of the organization’s leadership must be youth (optional but recommended).

Required Documents & Submission Checklist

To prepare your application, make sure you have the following:

For Individuals

1. Valid means of identification (e.g., NIN, PVC, passport)
2. Letter of intent or personal statement
3. Project or initiative description
4. Budget or funding request breakdown
5. Evidence of community engagement or impact (reports/photos)

For Organizations

1. CAC registration certificate
2. Organizational profile & vision statement
3. Project proposal with clear outcomes & activities
4. Budget plan and measurable indicators of success
5. Letters of support or references

What You Stand to Gain (Benefits)

Direct Benefits to Successful Applicants:

• Grant Funding: Support ranging from micro-grants to larger project funding
• Leadership & Advocacy Training
• Policy Engagement Opportunities
• Technical and Mentorship Support
• Networking with influential youth leaders across Nigeria
• Community recognition and media exposure
• Access to exclusive retreats, workshops, and learning exchanges

Whether you’re shaping civic policy, driving grassroots development, or scaling an innovation hub, NYFF helps amplify youth impact.

How to Apply (Step-by-Step)

Step 1: Visit the official NYFF portal here

Step 2: Navigate to the Grants section or specific programme page.

Step 3: Review eligibility and deadlines posted online.

Step 4: Click APPLY NOW and complete the online application form.

Step 5: Upload all required documents and submit before the deadline.

Step 6: Save your application reference and watch for confirmation emails.

Pro Tip: Submit early — this avoids last-minute technical issues and ensures your application is complete.

Key Deadlines & Application Windows (2025–2026)

Program Deadline Status
Youth Leadership Development Programme (YLDP) 31st March 2026 See portal
Youth Leadership Development Fund Grants (2026 Cycle) TBC (Applications underway) See portal
NYFF Grants Open Window Ongoing; deadlines usually quarterly See live portal

Important: Deadlines for specific grants vary by cycle — always check the NYFF portal for real-time updates and subscribe for email alerts.

Top Tips to Guarantee a Winning Application

1. Write a clear, measurable project outline.
2. Align your idea with NYFF thematic areas: governance, youth leadership, civic engagement.
3. Demonstrate measurable community impact.
4. Always double-check documents before uploading.
5. Follow NYFF on social media and subscribe for real-time updates.

Whether you’re a student leader, activist, entrepreneur, or youth-led organization, the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund is one of the most powerful platforms available to young Nigerians today. With its combination of grants, training, mentorship, and civic engagement opportunities, this is your chance to accelerate your impact — and build a brighter future for yourself and Nigeria.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Nigeria Youth Futures Fund – Empowering Tomorrow’s Leaders With Grants, Training and Policy Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Young Media Makers Fellowship 2026 – Training, Mentorship & Professional Growth for Emerging Media Creators
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Young Media Makers Fellowship Program 2026–2028 by DW Akademie is a long-term capacity-building initiative designed for emerging media professionals in the Western Balkans. The program brings together 12 young media professionals for an intensive multi-year journey combining training, mentorship, study visits, and hands-on media innovation. Funded by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation […]

The post Young Media Makers Fellowship 2026 – Training, Mentorship & Professional Growth for Emerging Media Creators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Young Media Makers Fellowship Program 2026–2028 by DW Akademie is a long-term capacity-building initiative designed for emerging media professionals in the Western Balkans. The program brings together 12 young media professionals for an intensive multi-year journey combining training, mentorship, study visits, and hands-on media innovation.

Funded by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), the fellowship equips participants with practical tools to create innovative media formats that engage young audiences — especially in rural areas.

What the Program Offers

The Young Media Makers Fellowship is a structured, long-term professional development experience running from May 2026 until the end of 2028.

The program includes:

  • 12 modules combining online and in-person seminars
  • Workshops on innovative media formats
  • Mentoring support for media product development
  • Regional and EU study visits
  • Capacity building in Media Information Literacy, AI, and digital innovation
  • Training in mobile journalism, TikTok and Instagram reporting, podcasting
  • Sessions on sustainable business models, project management, and media consulting

The fellowship begins with a mandatory kick-off module in Belgrade from 30 May – 3 June 2026. Participation in all 12 modules is required, although limited flexibility may be granted in urgent circumstances.

Approximately half of the modules will be online, and the remaining sessions will take place in person within Western Balkan countries or in EU countries.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Across the Western Balkans, young audiences are increasingly engaging with media through digital-first platforms — yet access to high-quality, locally relevant content remains uneven, particularly in rural areas.

For media professionals, this shift presents both pressure and possibility. The ability to create engaging, sustainable, and youth-centered formats is becoming essential — not optional.

This fellowship matters because it offers something rare: time and structure to grow strategically.

Instead of a short-term workshop, participants engage in a multi-year learning journey. They gain space to test ideas, receive expert feedback, and strengthen both editorial and entrepreneurial skills. The program bridges creative production with leadership development — helping participants think beyond individual stories toward long-term media impact.

It also builds regional connections. By working alongside peers from neighboring countries and participating in study visits, fellows expand their professional networks and gain exposure to diverse media systems and approaches.

For professionals ready to move from executing content to shaping media ecosystems, the Young Media Makers Fellowship provides the tools, mentorship, and credibility to take that step.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is open to media professionals aged 25–35 from:

  • Albania
  • Bosnia and Herzegovina
  • Kosovo
  • Montenegro
  • North Macedonia
  • Serbia

Applicants must have experience in journalism, media-related work, content production, project management, or related fields. Experience working with young people is considered an advantage.

English is the working language of the entire program, and all applications must be submitted in English.

Applicants may apply even if they currently live outside their home country, provided they are citizens of one of the eligible countries and reside within the Western Balkans region.

Media Product Development Focus

A central component of the program is the development of a media product idea targeting young audiences in rural areas in the Western Balkans.

Applicants must submit a sketch (maximum 4,000 characters) outlining:

  1. The key problem or need affecting young people in rural areas
  2. The type of media product they intend to develop (podcast, social media format, video series, local platform, etc.)
  3. The target audience (age, location, interests) and engagement strategy

The fellowship aims to support implementation of selected ideas. However, concepts may evolve during mentoring and collaboration with fellow participants.

Fellows receive mentoring support and may receive limited financial or technical support from DW Akademie to help implement their projects.

Costs and Financial Support

Participation in the program is free of charge. DW Akademie covers:

  • Travel expenses
  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Visa costs (if applicable)

Participants who successfully complete all modules will receive an official certificate from DW Akademie.

Application Process

To apply, candidates must:

  1. Complete the official Application Form (including a motivation letter of max. 3,000 characters and the media product idea sketch of max. 4,000 characters).
  2. Send their CV in English (PDF, max. 10 MB) to:
    dw-akademie.western.balkans@dw.com
    Subject line: “Application for Young Media Makers (your home country)”

Application Deadline

Sunday, 15 March 2026, 11:59 pm CET

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews via MS Teams between 27 March and 2 April 2026. Final decisions are expected by the end of April 2026.

If you are interested, learn more and apply on the official DW Akademie website.

Looking for more Opportunities? Visit the Opportunities for Youth website.

The post Young Media Makers Fellowship 2026 – Training, Mentorship & Professional Growth for Emerging Media Creators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD in New Zealand 2026: Wellington Doctoral Scholarships (NZD $32,000 Stipend + Tuition)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
If you are planning to pursue a fully funded PhD in New Zealand, the Wellington Doctoral Scholarships 2026 at Victoria University of Wellington present an outstanding opportunity for both domestic and international students. This prestigious scholarship supports high-achieving doctoral candidates with comprehensive financial assistance, allowing them to focus fully on research and academic development. About […]

The post Fully Funded PhD in New Zealand 2026: Wellington Doctoral Scholarships (NZD $32,000 Stipend + Tuition) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are planning to pursue a fully funded PhD in New Zealand, the Wellington Doctoral Scholarships 2026 at Victoria University of Wellington present an outstanding opportunity for both domestic and international students.

This prestigious scholarship supports high-achieving doctoral candidates with comprehensive financial assistance, allowing them to focus fully on research and academic development.

About Victoria University of Wellington

Victoria University of Wellington is one of New Zealand’s leading research institutions, located in the capital city of Wellington. The university is globally recognized for its strong academic reputation, research excellence, and international collaborations.

PhD programs are available across a wide range of disciplines, including:

  • Science and Engineering

  • Humanities and Social Sciences

  • Business and Government

  • Law

  • Education

  • Creative Arts

The university provides a vibrant research environment supported by expert supervision and modern facilities.

What Is the Wellington Doctoral Scholarship?

The Wellington Doctoral Scholarship is a highly competitive funding award designed to support students enrolling in a full-time PhD at Victoria University of Wellington.

Scholarships are awarded based on:

  • Academic excellence

  • Research potential

  • Quality of research proposal

  • Academic references

The program is open to applicants from around the world.

Scholarship Benefits (Financial Coverage)

Successful candidates will receive:

  • NZD $32,000 per year (stipend)

  • Full domestic tuition fees coverage for up to 3 years

  • Access to research supervision and university facilities

The annual stipend is intended to support living expenses throughout the doctoral program.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the Wellington Doctoral Scholarship 2026, applicants must:

  • Apply for a full-time PhD at Victoria University of Wellington

  • Demonstrate strong academic achievement

  • Provide strong academic references

  • Submit a competitive research proposal

Both international and domestic students are eligible to apply.

Application Rounds & Deadlines

Applications are typically considered in three rounds each year:

  • 1 March

  • 1 July

  • 1 November

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early, especially international students who may require additional time for admission and visa processing.

How to Apply

The application process generally includes:

  1. Applying for admission into a PhD program

  2. Preparing a detailed research proposal

  3. Submitting academic transcripts and certificates

  4. Providing referee reports

  5. Completing the scholarship application through the university portal

It is strongly recommended to contact potential supervisors before submitting your application.

Why Study in New Zealand for a PhD?

New Zealand offers:

  • A globally recognized education system

  • High research standards

  • A safe and welcoming environment

  • Post-study work opportunities

  • Strong international academic networks

For research-focused students, it provides an ideal balance between academic excellence and quality of life.

The Wellington Doctoral Scholarships 2026 provide an exceptional opportunity for students seeking fully funded PhD study in New Zealand. With tuition support and a generous annual stipend, this scholarship enables candidates to focus on impactful research without significant financial pressure.

Prospective applicants should carefully review admission requirements and submit a strong, well-developed research proposal to maximize their chances of selection.

Discover more PhD opportunities Here.

The post Fully Funded PhD in New Zealand 2026: Wellington Doctoral Scholarships (NZD $32,000 Stipend + Tuition) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for Biodiversity Media Grants 2026 (Up to €12,000) – Earth Journalism Network Funding Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Media organizations working to strengthen biodiversity reporting can now apply for the Biodiversity Media Grants 2026, offered by Internews through its Earth Journalism Network (EJN). This funding opportunity will support three to four media organizations in low- and middle-income countries with grants ranging from €10,000 to €12,000 to improve the quality and quantity of biodiversity […]

The post Apply for Biodiversity Media Grants 2026 (Up to €12,000) – Earth Journalism Network Funding Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Media organizations working to strengthen biodiversity reporting can now apply for the Biodiversity Media Grants 2026, offered by Internews through its Earth Journalism Network (EJN).

This funding opportunity will support three to four media organizations in low- and middle-income countries with grants ranging from €10,000 to €12,000 to improve the quality and quantity of biodiversity journalism and build the capacity of reporters covering conservation issues.

Why Biodiversity Reporting Matters

According to the latest findings from the Intergovernmental Platform on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services, in 2023, $7.3 trillion was invested in activities directly harming biodiversity, compared to just $220 billion invested in environmental protection and restoration. This alarming 30:1 imbalance highlights the urgent need for informed public discourse and accountability.

Despite the severity of biodiversity loss, these issues remain underreported globally. EJN’s Biodiversity Media Initiative aims to close this gap by equipping journalists and media outlets with the resources, mentorship, and funding necessary to produce impactful reporting.

Grant Overview

Selected organizations will:

  • Receive €10,000–€12,000 in funding

  • Gain access to experienced mentors throughout the project

  • Implement initiatives that raise awareness of biodiversity threats and conservation solutions

  • Produce robust journalism outputs (minimum 10 stories unless combined with capacity-building activities)

Projects should focus on increasing public understanding of biodiversity challenges and solutions, especially among audiences in lower- and middle-income countries.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants include:

  • Media organizations

  • Journalist networks

  • Civil society organizations

  • Academic institutions

Applicants must be based in countries classified as low- or middle-income by the World Bank. Preference will be given to professional journalists, media outlets, and journalism schools.

Important notes:

  • Applications must be submitted in English.

  • Advocacy or political campaigning projects will not be considered.

  • Organizations funded by EJN in 2024 are not eligible.

  • Transparency about the use of generative AI in proposal preparation is required.

Priority Themes for 2026

EJN is particularly interested in proposals addressing one or more of the following topics:

  • Other Effective Area-Based Conservation Measures (OECMs) as alternatives contributing to the Global Biodiversity Framework’s 30×30 target

  • Business dependencies and impacts on nature

  • Collaborative conservation models involving communities, governments, and private sector actors

  • Nature-based solutions supporting biodiversity, climate mitigation, food security, and livelihoods

  • Unintended harmful effects of conservation policies

  • Interconnections between biodiversity and public health

Examples of Funded Activities

Projects may include:

  • In-depth investigative or data-driven reporting

  • Transboundary collaborative journalism initiatives

  • Workshops and field training for journalists

  • Development of e-learning tools and reporting resources

  • Creation of storytelling platforms

  • Establishment or strengthening of journalist networks

  • Innovative mapping, data visualization, or fact-checking tools

Grant Timeline

  • Application Deadline: March 28, 2026

  • Notification of Successful Applicants: April 2026

  • Project Start Date: May 2026

  • Project Completion Deadline: April 2027 (within 12 months)

Budget and Logistics

Applicants must submit:

  • A detailed Excel budget using the official template

  • Justification for all requested costs

While co-financing is encouraged, it is not mandatory. Equipment purchases are allowed but must represent a relatively small portion of the total budget and directly contribute to project outcomes.

For projects producing published content, Internews and its partners retain rights to edit and distribute funded materials.

Safety and Acknowledgement Requirements

Grantees are encouraged to develop risk management plans addressing physical, digital, and mental health safety. Relevant costs can be included in the project budget.

All published work must include the acknowledgement:
“This story was produced with support from Internews’ Earth Journalism Network.”

Selection Criteria

Applications will be reviewed by a panel of Internews staff and environmental journalism experts. Evaluation will be based on:

  • Overall proposal quality

  • Relevance to program objectives

  • Potential impact and effectiveness

  • Innovation

  • Financial feasibility

  • Organisational capacity

  • Geographic diversity

How to Apply

  1. Click  “Apply now” 

  2. Log in or create an account.

  3. Complete the online application form.

  4. Upload required supporting documents, including your detailed budget.

  5. Submit before March 28, 2026.

The Biodiversity Media Grants 2026 present a major opportunity for media organizations in biodiversity-rich or vulnerable regions to strengthen environmental journalism and amplify critical conservation stories.

If your organization is ready to deepen public understanding of biodiversity challenges and drive impactful reporting, this grant could provide both the funding and mentorship needed to make a difference.

Discover more grant Opportunities here.

The post Apply for Biodiversity Media Grants 2026 (Up to €12,000) – Earth Journalism Network Funding Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Political Management Training 2026 in Asia-Pacific (AI and Digitisation Focus)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Regional Competence Center for Social Democracy & Trade Union Cooperation in Asia-Pacific has officially opened applications for the 2026 Political Management Training (PMT) program. Fully supported and managed by Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES), this prestigious training equips emerging political leaders with the knowledge and skills needed to shape a just, inclusive, and democratic future in the […]

The post Fully Funded Political Management Training 2026 in Asia-Pacific (AI and Digitisation Focus) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Regional Competence Center for Social Democracy & Trade Union Cooperation in Asia-Pacific has officially opened applications for the 2026 Political Management Training (PMT) program. Fully supported and managed by Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES), this prestigious training equips emerging political leaders with the knowledge and skills needed to shape a just, inclusive, and democratic future in the Asia-Pacific region.

The 2026 edition focuses on a timely and transformative theme: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Digitalisation Governance.

About the Political Management Training (PMT)

The Political Management Training (PMT) is an annual leadership development program designed for emerging political decision-makers, policy thinkers, and change-makers committed to advancing social democracy.

Each year, PMT centers on a pressing regional issue. In 2026, the program will explore how AI and digitalisation are reshaping governance, democratic institutions, and political processes across Asia-Pacific.

The program brings together:

  • Emerging politicians and aspiring political leaders

  • Academics and policy experts

  • Media professionals

  • Trade union representatives

  • Civil society leaders

PMT has built a strong alumni network of over 200 professionals who now serve as politicians, policy advisors, and party workers at local, state, and national levels.

Program Structure and Format

The PMT consists of a ten-day in-person training, divided into two clusters:

Cluster 1: AI & Digitalisation Governance

A master class covering:

  • Fundamental pillars of AI governance

  • Digital transformation in political systems

  • Challenges facing democratic institutions

  • Interactive dialogues and practical debates

Cluster 2: Advanced Political Management Skills

Focused on strengthening leadership competencies, including:

  • Negotiation tactics

  • Empathic communication strategies

  • Strategic planning

  • Progressive governance approaches to AI

Participants will also engage in:

  • Online and face-to-face learning exchanges

  • Institutional and cultural visits

  • One-on-one dialogues

  • Alumni knowledge-sharing sessions

Training locations will be hosted in selected Asia-Pacific cities to encourage dynamic exchange and policy inspiration.

Program Timeline (2026)

  • 02 Feb – 15 Mar: Application Period

  • 02 – 15 Mar: Initial Screening (Rolling Basis)

  • 23 – 27 Mar: Posting of Shortlisted Applicants

  • 12 May: PMT Introduction & Administrative Briefing (via Zoom)

  • 19 – 23 May: PMT Cluster 1 – Singapore (TBA)

  • 12 – 16 Oct: PMT Cluster 2 – Manila (TBA)

Eligibility Criteria

The program will select 20–25 participants from across Asia-Pacific, particularly from countries where FES operates, including:

Australia, Bangladesh, India, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Japan, Mongolia, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, South Korea, Thailand, and Vietnam.

Applicants from other Asia-Pacific countries may also apply and will be considered subject to availability.

Applicants must:

  • Be 23–35 years old

  • Have worked or currently work on AI & Digitalisation

  • Be proficient in English (oral and written)

  • Be an aspiring political decision-maker (elective or career position)

  • Be committed to freedom, equality, social justice, and solidarity

  • Be willing and able to travel within Asia-Pacific

Applicants from diverse gender, class, and ethnic backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Requirements

All documents must be submitted to: futurehub.pmt@fes.de
Deadline: 15 March 2026 (Sunday), 11:59 PM Manila Time

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Required Documents

  1. Video Application

    • Format: 1080p MP4 (h.264 codec)

    • File name: App_FullName_Country

    • Maximum length: 100 seconds

  2. Curriculum Vitae

    • Format: PDF

    • File name: CV_FullName_Country

Video Application Instructions

Applicants must appear on camera and answer the following:

How do you see AI’s application in politics in your country in the next five years?
What are the major challenges for democratic political institutions (political parties, governments, inter-governmental bodies) in this envisioned AI future?
How should institutions respond?

Important Notes:

  • Keep the video under 100 seconds

  • Ensure high-quality audio and clear visuals

  • Avoid heavy editing or animation

  • Keep the format simple and focused on content

If generative AI tools were used to brainstorm or refine your response, include a one-sentence disclosure in your email explaining how AI was used and list the prompts utilized.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated as follows:

  • 35% – Quality of Content (Video)

  • 25% – Clarity of Video & Audio

  • 20% – Background in AI & Digitalisation (CV)

  • 20% – Background in Politics (CV)

Total: 100%

For more details about the training, please visit Here.

As AI rapidly transforms governance and public life, the need for politically grounded, ethically driven leaders has never been greater. The 2026 Political Management Training offers a rare opportunity to build both the technical and political competencies required to lead in this digital era.

If you are an emerging leader working at the intersection of politics and technology, this could be your next big step.

Discover more similar Opportunities here

 

The post Fully Funded Political Management Training 2026 in Asia-Pacific (AI and Digitisation Focus) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Prestigious Public Interest Fellowship 2026 in the USA (Stipend Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Public Interest Fellowship (TPIF) 2026 Stipend Fellowship Cohort, a highly respected two-year leadership program designed for recent graduates and young professionals interested in American constitutional government, public policy, and liberal democracy. The Public Interest Fellowship (TPIF) offers participants a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of governance, public service, […]

The post Prestigious Public Interest Fellowship 2026 in the USA (Stipend Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Public Interest Fellowship (TPIF) 2026 Stipend Fellowship Cohort, a highly respected two-year leadership program designed for recent graduates and young professionals interested in American constitutional government, public policy, and liberal democracy.

The Public Interest Fellowship (TPIF) offers participants a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of governance, public service, and political philosophy while gaining access to extensive networking opportunities with leading policymakers, scholars, and public figures.

Based in Washington, DC and New York City, the fellowship combines professional development, policy education, and intellectual engagement through seminars, retreats, and networking events.

The program is designed for individuals who want to build careers in public policy, government, law, journalism, and national security while strengthening their understanding of the traditions of freedom and constitutional democracy.

About the Public Interest Fellowship (TPIF)

The Public Interest Fellowship’s flagship program is a two-year fellowship that brings together a cohort of highly talented young professionals committed to public service and the principles of liberty and constitutional governance.

Participants engage in rigorous intellectual study alongside practical professional development. The fellowship creates a collaborative environment where fellows regularly explore key political texts, engage in policy discussions, and interact with experienced leaders from across the public and nonprofit sectors.

Fellows meet regularly to study foundational writings on modern liberal democracy and American constitutionalism, guided by leading scholars and policy experts. These discussions aim to strengthen participants’ understanding of political systems and contemporary policy challenges.

In addition to academic study, fellows attend policy-focused lectures addressing pressing domestic and international issues, including national security, economic policy, and governance.

Fellowship Structure

The fellowship runs for two years, with programming held from September to June each academic year.

Fellows participate in:

  • Twice-monthly academic seminars
  • Policy lectures on current national and international issues
  • Monthly dinners with prominent public figures
  • Professional development workshops
  • Networking opportunities with policy professionals

Participants are expected to complete approximately 75 pages of reading per week, covering both historical texts and contemporary policy analysis.

The program also includes multi-day academic retreats and policy trips, which allow fellows to further engage with scholars, policymakers, and fellow participants.

Full-Time Fellowship Option

The full-time track offers paid professional placements for fellows.

Participants typically complete two professional rotations, each lasting 9–12 months, with organizations involved in public policy, governance, research, or advocacy.

These placements are carefully selected by the fellowship leadership in consultation with fellows to ensure that each role provides meaningful professional experience and mentorship.

The full-time fellowship for 2027 will open later in the fall.

Stipend Fellowship Option

The stipend fellowship track is designed for professionals who already hold full-time positions in Washington, DC or New York City.

Instead of rotating professional placements, stipend fellows receive an annual stipend while participating fully in the fellowship’s academic and networking programming.

This track allows professionals to continue their careers while engaging in the intellectual and leadership components of the fellowship.

Applications for the 2026 stipend fellowship cohort are currently open.

Professional Development Opportunities

The fellowship also offers a variety of additional opportunities to help participants develop leadership and policy skills.

These include:

  • Speech and debate training
  • Media and communications training
  • Access to national policy conferences
  • Networking with policymakers and scholars
  • Mentorship from leaders in government and nonprofit sectors

Fellows also attend exclusive dinners with members of Congress, senior government staff, nonprofit leaders, and policy experts, providing valuable insights into real-world policy decision-making.

Ideal Candidates

The fellowship seeks individuals with strong intellectual curiosity, leadership potential, and a deep interest in public policy.

Applicants should be:

  • Recent college graduates or young professionals with up to five years of experience
  • Interested in public policy, constitutional governance, and democratic institutions
  • Strong writers and communicators
  • Creative thinkers capable of independent work
  • Demonstrating professionalism and leadership potential

Candidates with interest in the following areas are particularly encouraged to apply:

  • Politics and governance
  • Freedom of speech and rule of law
  • Education reform
  • Economic and domestic policy
  • Foreign policy and national security
  • Journalism and public discourse
  • U.S.–Israel relations

APPLY HERE

Location

The fellowship operates in two major policy centers in the United States:

  • Washington, DC
  • New York, NY

Duration

Two years

Programming runs September – June each year

Deadline

18 May 2026

For more opportunities click here

 

The post Prestigious Public Interest Fellowship 2026 in the USA (Stipend Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Being Initiative Funding 2026: Up to $1.5 Million CAD for Youth Mental Health Solutions in 12 Countries
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Organizations working to improve youth mental health can now apply for major funding through the Being Initiative Request for Proposals (RFP). Led by Grand Challenges Canada, this global funding opportunity supports innovative solutions that address the root causes of mental health challenges among young people. The initiative focuses on prevention, promotion of well-being, and strengthening […]

The post Being Initiative Funding 2026: Up to $1.5 Million CAD for Youth Mental Health Solutions in 12 Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to improve youth mental health can now apply for major funding through the Being Initiative Request for Proposals (RFP). Led by Grand Challenges Canada, this global funding opportunity supports innovative solutions that address the root causes of mental health challenges among young people.

The initiative focuses on prevention, promotion of well-being, and strengthening the environments where young people live, learn, and grow.

About the Being Initiative

The Being Initiative is a global mental health program that envisions a world where young people feel well and thrive. The initiative funds research, innovation, and ecosystem-building approaches aimed at improving youth mental health outcomes.

Rather than focusing only on treatment after mental health challenges arise, the initiative prioritizes prevention and early drivers of distress. It supports solutions that address the social, environmental, and structural factors affecting young people’s mental health.

Through its Transition to Scale (TTS) funding stream, the initiative invests in approaches that have already demonstrated effectiveness and are ready to expand their reach and impact.

Countries Eligible for the Being Initiative Funding

The Being Initiative is investing in projects operating in the following countries:

  • Colombia

  • Ecuador

  • Ghana

  • India

  • Indonesia

  • Morocco

  • Pakistan

  • Romania

  • Senegal

  • Sierra Leone

  • Tanzania

  • Vietnam

Projects funded under this initiative aim to strengthen the environments where young people live, learn, and grow while addressing the underlying drivers of youth distress.

Each country has specific funding priorities targeting one key driver of youth mental health challenges. Applicants should review the Request for Proposals to ensure their solution aligns with the country-specific priorities.

Available Funding

The Being Initiative will invest between $300,000 and $1,500,000 CAD in selected projects. Please refer to our ‘Our investment approach’ page for more details on how we define Transition to Scale.

This funding supports Transition to Scale innovations, meaning initiatives that:

  • Have already been proven effective

  • Have demonstrated measurable results

  • Are ready to be implemented or expanded at a larger scale

The goal is to scale evidence-based solutions that improve youth mental health and strengthen prevention systems.

Who Can Apply

Any legally recognized organization can apply for this funding opportunity.

Eligible applicants may include:

  • Nonprofit organizations

  • Civil society organizations

  • Research institutions

  • Social enterprises

  • Community-based organizations

  • Youth-led organizations

Priority will be given to youth-led organizations based in one of the 12 participating countries.

Types of Projects Supported

The Being Initiative seeks bold and scalable solutions focused on preventing youth mental health challenges and promoting well-being.

Projects should aim to:

  • Address the root causes of youth distress

  • Strengthen supportive environments for young people

  • Promote prevention and early intervention strategies

  • Scale interventions that have already demonstrated effectiveness

  • Improve mental health outcomes through evidence-based innovation

Applicants are encouraged to review the initiative’s investment approach for details on how Transition to Scale innovations are defined.

Application Deadline

All proposals must be submitted by:

  • March 31, 2026

  • 2:00 PM Eastern Time (ET)

  • 7:00 PM Greenwich Mean Time (GMT)

Late applications will not be accepted.

How to Apply

All proposals must be submitted through Grand Challenges Canada’s online application portal (Fluxx).

Application portal:
You must submit your application via GCC’s online application portal, Fluxx: https://gcc.fluxx.io. We will not accept applications submitted via any other channel. Please visit our ‘How to Apply’ page for guidance.

Important application requirements:

  • Applications submitted through email will not be accepted

  • Only submissions through the Fluxx portal will be considered

  • Applicants should review all eligibility criteria before applying

Key Documents for Applicants

Please read all of the eligibility criteria and competition details in the full Request for Proposals document before applying.

For more information about the Being initiative, please visit www.beinginitiative.org

For questions related to the content of this request for proposals, Please visit the Apply for Funding page on our website for:

  • Guidance on How to Apply 
  • Our investment approach
  • Innovator Resources 
  • For anything not addressed through the request for proposals, reach out to OpenCompetitions@grandchallenges.ca
  • For technical issues related to the Fluxx portal, which are not addressed in the Fluxx Reference Guide, reach out to fluxxsupport@grandchallenges.ca

All proposals must be submitted by March 31, 2026, at 2:00 P.M. ET (7:00 P.M. GMT).

You must submit your application via GCC’s online application portal, Fluxx: https://gcc.fluxx.io.

Applicant Information Session

Interested applicants can join an information session to learn more about the funding opportunity.

Information Session Details:

  • Date: Thursday, 12 March 2026

  • Time: 8:00 AM Eastern Time

  • Format: Online via Zoom

The session will provide guidance on the application process and allow participants to ask questions.

Additional Resources

For more information about the Being Initiative, visit:
www.beinginitiative.org

Applicants can also consult the following resources on the Grand Challenges Canada website:

  • Guidance on how to apply

  • The Being Initiative investment approach

  • Innovator resources

For inquiries related to the funding call:

Content-related questions:
OpenCompetitions@grandchallenges.ca

Technical support for the Fluxx portal:
fluxxsupport@grandchallenges.ca

Key Documents:

  • Request for proposals – English
  • Demande de propositions – Français
  • Stage one application questions – English
  • Questions relatives à la première étape de la demande – Français

Why This Funding Opportunity Matters

Youth mental health challenges are increasing globally, yet investments in prevention remain limited. The Being Initiative aims to address this gap by funding innovations that tackle the early drivers of mental health challenges before they escalate.

By supporting scalable and evidence-based solutions, the initiative seeks to strengthen youth mental health ecosystems and create environments where young people can thrive.

Organizations working in youth development, mental health innovation, education, and community-based prevention are encouraged to review the full Request for Proposals and submit their applications before the deadline.

The post Being Initiative Funding 2026: Up to $1.5 Million CAD for Youth Mental Health Solutions in 12 Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship 2026 in Nigeria (Fully Sponsored 5-Day Residential Programme)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship is now open for applications from young Nigerian innovators passionate about food value addition, agribusiness, and sustainable food systems. This transformative 5-day residential fellowship is organized by Farm Central Intelligence (FarmCI), supported by UNESCO and Nestlé through the “Because You Matter” Program, with technical expertise from Soilless […]

The post Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship 2026 in Nigeria (Fully Sponsored 5-Day Residential Programme) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship is now open for applications from young Nigerian innovators passionate about food value addition, agribusiness, and sustainable food systems.

This transformative 5-day residential fellowship is organized by Farm Central Intelligence (FarmCI), supported by UNESCO and Nestlé through the “Because You Matter” Program, with technical expertise from Soilless Farm Lab.

The fellowship is designed to equip young Nigerians with practical, hands-on skills to transform raw agricultural produce—especially vegetables, spices, and peppers—into high-value, market-ready products.

Application deadline: 10th March, 2026.

About the Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship

Nigeria faces two major interconnected challenges: high post-harvest food losses and youth unemployment. Large quantities of agricultural produce are wasted due to poor processing, preservation, and storage systems. At the same time, many young people struggle to access meaningful employment and entrepreneurial opportunities.

The Feeding the Next Billion Fellowship addresses these challenges by:

  • Promoting food value addition

  • Reducing post-harvest losses

  • Building youth-led food enterprises

  • Strengthening local food systems

  • Enhancing national food security

Through a carefully structured 5-day immersive residential experience, participants gain practical skills in processing, packaging, branding, and basic business development.

The program fosters not only technical competence but also an entrepreneurial mindset rooted in sustainability and community impact.

What Fellows Will Learn

The fellowship curriculum focuses on transforming raw agricultural produce into profitable, market-ready products.

Key training areas include:

1. Food Processing and Preservation

  • Converting vegetables, spices, and peppers into value-added products

  • Extending shelf life through preservation techniques

  • Reducing spoilage and waste

2. Packaging and Branding

  • Product presentation

  • Market positioning

  • Brand identity development

  • Understanding consumer preferences

3. Basic Business Management

  • Business planning

  • Costing and pricing

  • Market analysis

  • Financial fundamentals

4. Sustainable Practices

  • Reducing food waste

  • Promoting circular economy principles

  • Supporting local value chains

The hands-on nature of the training ensures participants leave with practical, actionable skills rather than just theoretical knowledge.

Participant Incentives and Full Support Package

Selected fellows receive a comprehensive support package designed to remove financial barriers and accelerate their entrepreneurial journey.

Fully Sponsored Residential Training

All costs for the 5-day intensive residential training in Ogun State are covered, including:

  • Accommodation

  • Meals

  • Training materials

  • Access to facilities at Soilless Farm Lab

Transportation Support

Participants receive financial assistance to cover transportation to and from the training venue.

Hands-On Mentorship

Fellows gain direct access to:

  • Expert trainers from FarmCI

  • Technical specialists from Soilless Farm Lab

  • Industry mentors with practical agribusiness experience

Personalized Starter Kit

Each fellow receives a starter kit containing essential tools and materials to begin implementing their food value addition business immediately after the program.

This practical support ensures participants can transition from learning to implementation without delay.

Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of a growing ecosystem of:

  • Food innovators

  • Agribusiness entrepreneurs

  • Industry partners

  • Program alumni

This network creates long-term collaboration and peer support opportunities.

Certificate of Completion

Upon successful completion, fellows receive formal recognition certifying their participation and training.

Post-Program Support

The fellowship does not end after the 5-day residential training. Participants receive:

  • Continued mentorship

  • Business development guidance

  • Follow-up support

  • Opportunities to share success stories

Programme Timeline: What to Expect

The fellowship journey is structured into four key phases.

Phase 1: Application and Selection

  • Official program announcement

  • Application submission

  • Review and screening process

  • Acceptance notification for selected candidates

Phase 2: Pre-Fellowship Preparation

Selected fellows receive:

  • Onboarding communication

  • Detailed program information

  • Guidance on preparation

  • Logistics and travel details

This ensures participants are fully prepared for the immersive residential experience.

Phase 3: The 5-Day Residential Fellowship

Held at Soilless Farm Lab in Ogun State, this is the core of the program.

Participants engage in:

  • Hands-on workshops

  • Practical food processing sessions

  • Mentorship sessions

  • Group collaboration

  • Entrepreneurial mindset development

This immersive environment encourages intensive learning and peer exchange.

Phase 4: Post-Fellowship Growth and Support

After the training:

  • Fellows receive their starter kits

  • Certificates are awarded

  • Mentorship continues

  • Business implementation begins

  • Progress follow-ups are conducted

This structured support increases the likelihood of real business creation and sustainability.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is designed for young Nigerian innovators passionate about:

  • Food processing

  • Agribusiness

  • Entrepreneurship

  • Sustainable food systems

  • Reducing food waste

Special encouragement is given to youths from:

  • Lagos State

  • Ogun State

  • Oyo State

However, the program welcomes applicants from across Nigeria.

The initiative promotes inclusivity and diversity in building Nigeria’s next generation of food entrepreneurs.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Food value addition is a powerful economic lever. Instead of selling raw produce at low prices, entrepreneurs can:

  • Increase product value

  • Create jobs

  • Build local brands

  • Reduce waste

  • Improve income stability

By empowering young Nigerians with skills and tools, the Feeding the Next Billion Fellowship contributes to:

  • Youth employment

  • Economic resilience

  • Food security

  • Sustainable agriculture

  • Local enterprise growth

Click HERE to learn more and apply.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship 2026 in Nigeria (Fully Sponsored 5-Day Residential Programme) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Capacity-Building Programme for Women Energy Entrepreneurs in SADC 2026: Apply by 3 April 2026 for the Second Cohort
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Second Cohort of the Capacity-Building Programme for Women Energy Entrepreneurs in the SADC region is now open for applications. This six-month entrepreneurship development initiative is designed to strengthen the leadership, business growth, and financing readiness of women-led energy enterprises across Southern Africa. The programme is led by the Global Women’s Network for the Energy […]

The post Capacity-Building Programme for Women Energy Entrepreneurs in SADC 2026: Apply by 3 April 2026 for the Second Cohort appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Second Cohort of the Capacity-Building Programme for Women Energy Entrepreneurs in the SADC region is now open for applications. This six-month entrepreneurship development initiative is designed to strengthen the leadership, business growth, and financing readiness of women-led energy enterprises across Southern Africa.

The programme is led by the Global Women’s Network for the Energy Transition (GWNET) in collaboration with the SADC Centre for Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency (SACREEE), with financial support from the Austrian Development Agency (ADA), the operational unit of the International Partnership Austria.

This initiative focuses on empowering women entrepreneurs to accelerate sustainable energy solutions throughout the SADC region.

About the Capacity-Building Programme

The 6-month programme is designed to support women entrepreneurs who own or lead energy-related businesses in strengthening their enterprises through soft-skill business development training, mentorship, and networking.

The programme aims to:

  • Advance women-led businesses in the energy sector

  • Strengthen entrepreneurial and leadership capacities

  • Facilitate access to financing through matchmaking and funder connections

  • Enhance visibility and professional networks

  • Contribute to the clean energy transition in the SADC region

It is important to note that the programme does not provide capital funding directly. Instead, it facilitates enterprise financing through structured matchmaking and introductions to potential investors and financial institutions.

Why This Programme Matters

Women entrepreneurs remain underrepresented in the renewable energy and energy efficiency sectors. By strengthening women-led businesses, the programme contributes to:

  • Regional economic empowerment

  • Clean energy access

  • Sustainable development

  • Gender equality in the energy sector

Through structured support, mentorship, and financing linkages, the initiative equips women entrepreneurs to drive sustainable energy innovation in Southern Africa.

Programme Structure and Duration

Duration: 6 months
Mode: Fully virtual / online
Time Commitment: Approximately 8 hours per month

Participants must be able to commit to virtual engagement and attend scheduled sessions throughout the duration of the programme.

The training focuses exclusively on business and entrepreneurial soft skills. It does not include technical training in energy technologies.

Areas of Focus

The programme welcomes women entrepreneurs from across the energy value chain. However, preferential consideration will be given to businesses operating in:

  • Solar thermal solutions

  • Energy efficiency

  • Productive use of energy

The broader objective is to empower women-led enterprises that contribute to sustainable energy development and regional energy transformation.

Benefits of the Programme

Selected participants will gain:

1. Entrepreneurship and Leadership Development

Structured training modules designed to strengthen:

  • Business strategy

  • Financial management

  • Market positioning

  • Growth planning

  • Leadership and communication skills

2. Access to Industry Experts and Role Models

Participants will engage with experienced professionals and industry leaders who serve as mentors and role models within the renewable energy and entrepreneurship ecosystem.

3. Networking and Community Building

The programme connects women entrepreneurs across the SADC region, fostering:

  • Peer learning

  • Collaboration

  • Cross-border business opportunities

  • Regional support networks

4. Access to Enterprise Financing Opportunities

Although the programme does not provide direct capital funding, it facilitates:

  • Matchmaking with potential funders

  • Exposure to financing institutions

  • Support in preparing for funding discussions

This structured approach increases participants’ readiness to attract investment and scale their businesses.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the programme, applicants must:

  • Identify as a woman

  • Hold nationality from a SADC Member State

  • Be an entrepreneur or owner of an energy-related business

  • Be able to commit approximately 8 hours per month to virtual participation

  • Complete the application in English

The programme is exclusively open to women with SADC nationality.

Application Process

Interested applicants must complete the official online application form:

Application Form: https://forms.gle/J1kHChM5vtBsJXLT8

Full details:
https://www.sacreee.org/opportunity/call-applications-second-cohort-capacity-building-programme-women-energy-entrepreneurs

Project details:
https://www.sacreee.org/project/empowering-women-advance-energy-transition-sadc-region

Application Period: 2 March 2026 – 3 April 2026
Deadline: 3 April 2026 (23:59 CAT)

Applications must be completed in English.

Incomplete or late applications will not be considered.

For inquiries: energy@sacreee.org

About the Organizers

The programme is delivered through collaboration between:

  • Global Women’s Network for the Energy Transition (GWNET)

  • SADC Centre for Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency (SACREEE)

  • Austrian Development Agency (ADA)

The SADC Centre for Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency (SACREEE) is based in Windhoek, Namibia and works to promote renewable energy and energy efficiency across the SADC region.

This initiative forms part of broader efforts to promote gender equality in the clean energy transition.

Discover more fellowship opportunities HERE.

The post Capacity-Building Programme for Women Energy Entrepreneurs in SADC 2026: Apply by 3 April 2026 for the Second Cohort appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
